summary: Cleaned up game-mode code by naming a registration/common helper, replacing several magic character and fighter constants with enum names, introducing CKIND_PLAYABLE_COUNT for playable-character bounds, and renaming the StartMeleeRules team flag from x8/x6-style fields to is_teams. The PR also implemented gm_8017BE8C as a full match and implemented/renamed gm_8017BEC8 as gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable, improving it to 98.44% matched according to the decomp.dev bot.
what_changed: Renamed symbol gm_8017BEC8 to gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable in config/GALE01/symbols.txt and updated callers/prototypes.
Implemented gm_8017BE8C in gmregcommon.c as a loop over three opponents, counting entries not equal to CHKIND_NONE.

Implemented gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable in gmregcommon.c using CKIND_NONE/CHKIND_NONE-style filtering, a NUM_OPPONENTS constant of 3, color conflict detection, 0xFF as a temporary invalid-color marker, and OSReport/__assert on failure.
Changed gmregcommon.h from placeholder/UNK_RET-style declarations to typed declarations for gm_8017BE84, gm_8017BE8C, and gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable.
Removed the local struct gm_804DAC18_t usage from gmadventure.c and replaced it with explicit s8 ckinds[3] and u8 colors[3] arrays passed to gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable.
Added CKIND_PLAYABLE_COUNT to CharacterKind in ft/forward.h and made CKIND_MASTERH equal to that count, giving code a named boundary between playable and non-playable character kinds.
Replaced playable-character loop bounds and magic character checks in ftlib.c with CKIND_PLAYABLE_COUNT, CKIND_ZELDA, and CKIND_SEAK.
Named lbl_803D51A0[0] as ncolors and sized the table as CKIND_PLAYABLE_COUNT in gm_1601.c; accessor arguments were renamed from arg0 to ckind.
Converted a static character-kind table in gm_1601.static.h from raw numeric values to CKIND_* enum constants.
Renamed StartMeleeRules.x8 and lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t.x6 team-mode fields to is_teams across gm, mn, and related files.

Replaced many raw character/fighter constants with named constants such as CKIND_MARS, CKIND_GAMEWATCH, CKIND_FALCO, CKIND_CLINK, FTKIND_NONE, FTKIND_YOSHI, FTKIND_LINK, FTKIND_KIRBY, FTKIND_PICHU, FTKIND_PURIN, FTKIND_POPO, FTKIND_NANA, FTKIND_BOY, and FTKIND_GIRL.
Updated Player_800325C8 parameter naming/type from slot to CharacterKind kind, matching its use as an index into ftMapping_list.
Changed mn_8022E950 return type from int to bool because it returns the result of gm_80164840.
Changed gmstaffroll gm_801AA664 parameter type from int to CharacterKind and included ft/forward.h.
smart_moves: Introduced CKIND_PLAYABLE_COUNT instead of leaving the playable range encoded as <= 25, which makes loops safer when non-playable character kinds follow the playable block.
Kept CKIND_MASTERH numerically stable by assigning it to CKIND_PLAYABLE_COUNT rather than inserting a count enumerator that would shift later enum values.
Used named CKIND_ and FTKIND_ constants to document whether a value is a character kind or fighter kind, reducing ambiguity in gm/adventure/stage logic.
Renamed x8/x6 team-mode fields to is_teams and changed local temporaries to bool, making boolean intent explicit without changing call structure.
Replaced an anonymous three-byte struct used for enemy colors/kinds with separate ckinds and colors arrays, which better matches gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable's actual interface.
Kept gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable constrained by NUM_OPPONENTS rather than scattering the literal 3 throughout the implementation.
Preserved assertions/error reporting in the color-selection helper, making the decompiled control flow explicit while guarding impossible color-selection states.
decomp_lessons: When an enum has a meaningful subrange, add a named count sentinel and explicitly pin the next enum value to avoid accidental ABI/value shifts.
Replace magic character/fighter IDs only after confirming whether the context expects CKIND_* or FTKIND_*; this PR shows both kinds in nearby gm code.
Field names like is_teams can be propagated across StartMeleeRules, copied match-result structs, and menu/game-mode users to reveal shared semantics.
A small matched helper can unlock larger partial matches: gm_8017BE8C reached 100% and gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable improved to 98.44% in the same unit.
For decomp cleanup, arrays with typed element names can be clearer than placeholder local structs when the data is semantically parallel lists, as in ckinds/colors.
Use CHKIND_NONE or FTKIND_NONE instead of raw 0x21 where the sentinel's domain is known.
Changing prototype parameter names and types can be valuable documentation even without altering behavior, as with Player_800325C8(CharacterKind kind, bool b).
naming_conventions: Unknown gm_* symbols may be promoted to descriptive camel-case-with-prefix names when behavior is understood, e.g. gm_8017BEC8 -> gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable.
Boolean struct fields should use is_* names when evidence supports boolean semantics, e.g. is_teams.
Character-kind parameters are named ckind or ckind0/ckind1/ckind2 rather than generic arg0 when they index character-kind tables.
Playable-character boundary constants use *_COUNT naming, e.g. CKIND_PLAYABLE_COUNT.
No-character sentinels should use domain-specific names such as CHKIND_NONE or FTKIND_NONE rather than raw 0x21.
Local arrays should be named by contents, e.g. ckinds and colors, instead of placeholder stack struct names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: gm_8017BE8C was implemented as a simple counted loop over NUM_OPPONENTS entries, incrementing for each opponent not equal to CHKIND_NONE; decomp.dev reported it as a new 100% match worth +60 bytes.

gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable was implemented with loop variables ordered as k, j, i declarations and nested loops over opponent slots/colors; decomp.dev reported the function at 98.44% matched, +504 bytes improved from 0%.

The color-table helper marks conflicting same-character/same-color entries with 0xFF, searches for a non-player/non-conflicting color among gm_80169238(ckind) available colors, and asserts if none is found.
Magic-number replacement was done in a behavior-preserving way: constants were substituted with enum names, and CKIND_MASTERH was explicitly assigned to preserve numeric layout.
Type cleanup avoided broad rewrites: many edits changed field names, bool comparisons, or prototypes while keeping original control flow and matching-sensitive structure intact.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code unchanged overall at 41.34% but with +60 bytes matched.
decomp.dev bot reported one new match: main/melee/gm/gmregcommon gm_8017BE8C, 0.00% to 100.00%, +60 bytes.
decomp.dev bot reported one unmatched-function improvement: main/melee/gm/gmregcommon gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable, 0.00% to 98.44%, +504 bytes.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable from 98.44% to 100%.
Search for remaining StartMeleeRules.x8 or lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t.x6 references to ensure is_teams naming propagated fully.
Search for remaining raw 0x21 character/fighter sentinels in gm, ft, mn, and gr code and replace with CHKIND_NONE or FTKIND_NONE where domain is clear.
Search for loops over hard-coded playable character ranges such as <= 25 and replace with CKIND_PLAYABLE_COUNT where appropriate.
Audit remaining gm_80164840 callers using numeric literals and convert to CKIND_* names when the values are character kinds.
